canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Food-101 is consistently presented as a measurement instrument for the task of image classification. It is defined as a "challenging data set of 101 food categories, with 101,000 images for image classification" ("CoVoGER: A Multilingual Multitask Benchmark for Speech-to-text Generative Error Correction with Large Language Models"). The papers in this set use Food-101 to operationalize the evaluation of image or vision classification capabilities. It is frequently employed alongside other datasets such as CIFAR-100, Flowers-102, RESISC45, and Tiny-Imagenet. This common grouping suggests its role as a tool within a suite of benchmarks for assessing this specific machine learning behavior.
papers: CoVoGER: A Multilingual Multitask Benchmark for Speech-to-text Generative Error Correction with Large Language Models
Efficient Unstructured Pruning of Mamba State-Space Models for Resource-Constrained Environments
NESTFUL: A Benchmark for EvaluatingLLMs on Nested Sequences ofAPICalls

definitions.text: The Food-101 dataset is a challenging data set of 101 food categories, with 101,000 images for image classification.
definitions.paper: CoVoGER: A Multilingual Multitask Benchmark for Speech-to-text Generative Error Correction with Large Language Models

relationships.paper: Efficient Unstructured Pruning of Mamba State-Space Models for Resource-Constrained Environments
relationships.evidence: vision classification with CIFAR100 (Krizhevsky et al., 2009), Food-101 (Bossard et al., 2014), Flowers-102 (Nilsback and Zisserman, 2008) and RESISC45 (Cheng et al., 2017)
